title: e2e: GetPort(): never reuse ports
body:
Intended to fix an obscure, unlikely race condition in which (I
think) two parallel jobs called GetPort() and were assigned
the same port.

[test log](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5857246123393024/html/int-podman-debian-12-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--podman-search-doesnt-attempt-HTTP-if-registry-is-not-listed-as-insecure--1)

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@Luap99 is this better? I followed the mutex convention used in `processTestResult()`. 

I am deliberately not addressing your second comment ("not just localhost") because I don't see the use case.

Unless you use something with inter process communication it is not going to work in parallel testing, processTestResult is only storing stuff for the single process, i.e. we can actually drop the mutex there: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18163/commits/5eb99a0ac88a6d65d11d307c87043f42b0df07be

One way would be to write files into a shared dir, see `LockTmpDir`.
But maybe the better way is to drop this at all do not use some free random from the `/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_local_port_range` like it does right now. These ports are bound dynamically by processes which makes conflicts more likely then lets say bind some port below that.

So I would say it is better to follow what we do in the system tests. Pick a random port in some fixed range (e.g. 5000-6000) then test if it is free then use it. To prevent the TOCTOU race between the parallel ginkgo processes the port should fulfil the condition `port % GinkgoT().ParallelTotal() == GinkgoT().ParallelProcess() - 1` (ParallelProcess is 1-indexed so we need to subtract one)
